Restored vintage random-width solid American cherry hardwood flooring in an Encinitas estate kitchen after wax removal, selective sanding, stain matching and Bona Traffic HD satin finishing. Vintage random-width American cherry flooring restored in an expansive Encinitas home, seen here from the kitchen island looking into the dining room. Decades of fading, wax, scratches and wear were corrected through selective sanding, deep cleaning, color matching, clear sealing and two coats of Bona Traffic HD in a commercial satin sheen.
caption: Vintage random-width American cherry flooring restored in an expansive Encinitas home, seen here from the kitchen island looking into the dining room. Decades of fading, wax, scratches and wear were corrected through selective sanding, deep cleaning, color matching, clear sealing and two coats of Bona Traffic HD in a commercial satin sheen.

alt: Completed American cherry hardwood-floor restoration in an Encinitas dining room with random-width solid planks, matched stain, clear Bona sealer and commercial-satin Traffic HD finish. A second completed view of the Encinitas American cherry restoration, from the coffered-ceiling dining room looking toward the kitchen and stone fireplace. The worn, wax-contaminated floor was selectively sanded, cleaned, matched to the surrounding cherry and protected with a clear Bona sealer and commercial-satin Traffic HD finish.
caption: A second completed view of the Encinitas American cherry restoration, from the coffered-ceiling dining room looking toward the kitchen and stone fireplace. The worn, wax-contaminated floor was selectively sanded, cleaned, matched to the surrounding cherry and protected with a clear Bona sealer and commercial-satin Traffic HD finish.

alt: Four-inch solid maple flooring restored in a Point Loma great room overlooking downtown San Diego and San Diego Bay after de-waxing, dust-contained sanding, creamy color treatment and matte water-based finishing. Four-inch solid maple flooring restored in a Point Loma residence, with the great-room windows framing the marina, downtown skyline and San Diego Bay. Heavy sun fading, wax, polish, scratches and wear were removed before the floor received a modern creamy tone and an extra-durable matte water-based finish.
caption: Four-inch solid maple flooring restored in a Point Loma residence, with the great-room windows framing the marina, downtown skyline and San Diego Bay. Heavy sun fading, wax, polish, scratches and wear were removed before the floor received a modern creamy tone and an extra-durable matte water-based finish.

alt: Finished creamy-tone solid maple hardwood floor in Point Loma viewed from the staircase after precision planetary sanding, gap repair and matte commercial-grade water-based polyurethane. Another completed view of the Point Loma maple project, seen from the staircase with the terrace doors open to the bay. Precision sanding and planetary refinement produced a flat surface, while the selected creamy tone and matte commercial-grade finish gave the decades-old floor a clean modern appearance.
caption: Another completed view of the Point Loma maple project, seen from the staircase with the terrace doors open to the bay. Precision sanding and planetary refinement produced a flat surface, while the selected creamy tone and matte commercial-grade finish gave the decades-old floor a clean modern appearance.

alt: Restored solid maple floor in a Point Loma ocean-view dining room with creamy color, flat planetary-sanded surface and ultra-durable matte water-based finish. Final view of the restored Point Loma solid maple flooring in the dining area with its mirrored wall and chandelier. The original floor was cleaned, decontaminated and sanded with full dust containment before receiving the homeowners' chosen creamy tone and a durable matte finish.
caption: Final view of the restored Point Loma solid maple flooring in the dining area with its mirrored wall and chandelier. The original floor was cleaned, decontaminated and sanded with full dust containment before receiving the homeowners' chosen creamy tone and a durable matte finish.

alt: Restored three-inch rift-and-quartered select white oak flooring in Bird Rock after dust-contained sanding, clear sealing and satin commercial-grade water-based finishing. Vintage Graf Brothers rift-and-quartered white oak restored in a coastal Bird Rock home. The floors and stairs were sanded with double-HEPA dust containment, sealed clear to preserve the natural white-oak color and protected with a commercial-grade satin water-based finish.
caption: Vintage Graf Brothers rift-and-quartered white oak restored in a coastal Bird Rock home. The floors and stairs were sanded with double-HEPA dust containment, sealed clear to preserve the natural white-oak color and protected with a commercial-grade satin water-based finish.

alt: Restored random-width wide-plank character hickory flooring in a Carmel Valley hallway after water damage, cupping, heavy ambering and wear were removed with dust-contained sanding and matte Bona finishing. Wide-plank character hickory restored in a Carmel Valley home, seen along the hallway beneath the wrought-iron chandelier. After cupping, water damage, scratches, fading and heavy ambering, the floor was sanded flat, clear sealed and finished with a matte Bona water-based polyurethane.
caption: Wide-plank character hickory restored in a Carmel Valley home, seen along the hallway beneath the wrought-iron chandelier. After cupping, water damage, scratches, fading and heavy ambering, the floor was sanded flat, clear sealed and finished with a matte Bona water-based polyurethane.

alt: Completed Carmel Valley character hickory restoration in the great room, with random-width planks, clear Bona sealer and matte water-based polyurethane after cupping and water damage. A second completed view of the Carmel Valley hickory project in the great room with its stone fireplace and French doors, showing the natural variation of the random-width character planks after precision sanding, clear sealing and matte Bona protection.
caption: A second completed view of the Carmel Valley hickory project in the great room with its stone fireplace and French doors, showing the natural variation of the random-width character planks after precision sanding, clear sealing and matte Bona protection.

alt: Five-inch maple flooring in Del Mar restored with gap filling, white stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw in an ultra-low sheen, seen from the front entry with the ocean beyond. Five-inch maple flooring transformed in a Del Mar ocean-view home, seen from the front entry beneath the vaulted plank ceiling with the glass stair rail at right and the ocean beyond the sliders. After sanding to raw wood and filling gaps and cracks, the floor received a white stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw for a soft, ultra-low-sheen finish.
caption: Five-inch maple flooring transformed in a Del Mar ocean-view home, seen from the front entry beneath the vaulted plank ceiling with the glass stair rail at right and the ocean beyond the sliders. After sanding to raw wood and filling gaps and cracks, the floor received a white stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw for a soft, ultra-low-sheen finish.

alt: Solid ash flooring installed at 45 degrees in a Del Mar ocean-view home after de-waxing, dust-contained sanding and clear natural finishing. Solid ash flooring overlooking Torrey Pines Beach restored after years of wax, polish, sun, water and coastal exposure. The 45-degree floor was cleaned, sanded flat and finished clear so the natural ash grain remained the focus.
caption: Solid ash flooring overlooking Torrey Pines Beach restored after years of wax, polish, sun, water and coastal exposure. The 45-degree floor was cleaned, sanded flat and finished clear so the natural ash grain remained the focus.

alt: Engineered red oak flooring restored across two levels with a custom stain that highlights the grain and a matte Bona Traffic water-based finish. caption: Engineered red oak restored throughout a split-level residence. A custom stain was selected to emphasize the red-oak grain, followed by a durable matte Bona Traffic finish.

alt: Red oak flooring in the Carmel Valley–Del Mar area with new boards installed to match, then sanded together and finished in a custom provincial stain. The entire surface was then sanded, stained in a custom provincial tone and finished together for a consistent result.
caption: New red oak flooring was installed to match the existing floor in this Carmel Valley–Del Mar home. The entire surface was then sanded, stained in a custom provincial tone and finished together for a consistent result.

caption: The final coat is still wet on this expansive Rancho Santa Fe entryway. Vintage 2¼-inch red oak, likely dating to the 1960s, was restored in a natural color and protected with a fresh finish.

caption: New engineered red oak was installed in one room and matched to the existing floor in this Encinitas–Carlsbad home. Clear sealer and clear finish preserved a restrained, natural red-oak appearance.

alt: Wide-plank fumed engineered French oak restored in the Galleher flooring showroom in Kearny Mesa, San Diego. Wide-plank fumed French oak restored for the Galleher flooring showroom in Kearny Mesa, with the sample displays and herringbone inlay visible across the sales floor. The completed work renews the showroom surface while preserving the character of the engineered oak.
caption: Wide-plank fumed French oak restored for the Galleher flooring showroom in Kearny Mesa, with the sample displays and herringbone inlay visible across the sales floor. The completed work renews the showroom surface while preserving the character of the engineered oak.

alt: Second completed view of restored wide-plank fumed French oak flooring in the Galleher showroom in Kearny Mesa. caption: Another completed view of the Galleher showroom restoration, looking across the renewed wide-plank fumed French oak toward the product displays and the herringbone field beneath the round pendant light.

alt: Alternate wet-finish view of restored engineered Brazilian cherry flooring in a First Avenue Coronado home overlooking San Diego Bay at dusk. Another view of the Coronado bayfront Brazilian cherry floor while the final coat is still wet, photographed at dusk with the amber accent lighting reflecting off the fresh finish and the lights of San Diego Bay beyond the First Avenue property.
caption: Another view of the Coronado bayfront Brazilian cherry floor while the final coat is still wet, photographed at dusk with the amber accent lighting reflecting off the fresh finish and the lights of San Diego Bay beyond the First Avenue property.

alt: Floor-level view of the Del Mar five-inch maple floor with white stain and ultra-low-sheen Bona Traffic HD Raw. A third completed view of the Del Mar maple restoration, at floor level looking toward the glass stair rail and open deck door, showing the soft white color and ultra-low-sheen Bona Traffic HD Raw finish.
caption: A third completed view of the Del Mar maple restoration, at floor level looking toward the glass stair rail and open deck door, showing the soft white color and ultra-low-sheen Bona Traffic HD Raw finish.

alt: Another completed view of triple-bleached engineered Brazilian cherry installed at 45 degrees in Solana Beach with off-white stain and low-sheen finish. A second view of the specialty Solana Beach Brazilian cherry project, in the living area beside the slate-tiled fireplace, showing the lightened appearance after triple bleaching, off-white stain and low-sheen finishing.
caption: A second view of the specialty Solana Beach Brazilian cherry project, in the living area beside the slate-tiled fireplace, showing the lightened appearance after triple bleaching, off-white stain and low-sheen finishing.

alt: Completed creamy-tone rift-and-quartered red oak flooring viewed from the front-door landing of a Fairbanks Ranch home. The Fairbanks Ranch rift-and-quartered red oak restoration viewed from the landing inside the front doors, past the curved staircase and through the arched opening, showing the completed creamy custom treatment throughout the architectural interior.
caption: The Fairbanks Ranch rift-and-quartered red oak restoration viewed from the landing inside the front doors, past the curved staircase and through the arched opening, showing the completed creamy custom treatment throughout the architectural interior.

alt: Seven-inch random-length engineered French oak flooring glued to a slab in La Jolla, sanded after installation and finished natural. Seven-inch square-edge French oak installed over a La Jolla concrete slab, then sanded and finished natural. The light-rustic grade and random lengths allow the character of the wide-plank floor to remain visible.
caption: Seven-inch square-edge French oak installed over a La Jolla concrete slab, then sanded and finished natural. The light-rustic grade and random lengths allow the character of the wide-plank floor to remain visible.

alt: Another completed view of the creamy custom rift-and-quartered red oak floor in Fairbanks Ranch North, Rancho Santa Fe. Another completed perspective of the Fairbanks Ranch rift-and-quartered red oak floor, in the expansive main room with its arched doorways and stone accents, after its transformation from a dark vintage stain to a creamy modern finish.
caption: Another completed perspective of the Fairbanks Ranch rift-and-quartered red oak floor, in the expansive main room with its arched doorways and stone accents, after its transformation from a dark vintage stain to a creamy modern finish.

alt: Solid merbau flooring restored in coastal Encinitas after sun fading, wax and grime, then sealed with oil sealer and finished matte. Solid merbau flooring restored in a coastal Encinitas home after years of sun fading, wax, polish and embedded grime. Oil sealer renewed the color before a matte protective finish was applied.
caption: Solid merbau flooring restored in a coastal Encinitas home after years of sun fading, wax, polish and embedded grime. Oil sealer renewed the color before a matte protective finish was applied.

alt: Maple flooring in the Carmel Valley–Del Mar area restored with precision sanding and a lightly ambering sealer. Completed maple restoration in the Carmel Valley–Del Mar area. The floor was sanded clean and treated with a sealer that added a restrained amber tone.
caption: Completed maple restoration in the Carmel Valley–Del Mar area. The floor was sanded clean and treated with a sealer that added a restrained amber tone.

alt: Upstairs Brazilian cherry hardwood flooring in a Coronado home after precision installation, sanding and finishing. Completed upstairs Brazilian cherry flooring in a Coronado residence, showing the result after careful installation, precision sanding and finishing.
caption: Completed upstairs Brazilian cherry flooring in a Coronado residence, showing the result after careful installation, precision sanding and finishing.

alt: Engineered walnut flooring restored in a San Elijo Hills home with precision refinishing and a renewed natural walnut appearance. Engineered walnut flooring after a complete refinishing project in San Elijo Hills. The restored surface brings back the depth and natural variation of the walnut.
caption: Engineered walnut flooring after a complete refinishing project in San Elijo Hills. The restored surface brings back the depth and natural variation of the walnut.

alt: Natural-finished 2¼-inch vintage white oak flooring restored in a Mission Hills home. Vintage 2¼-inch white oak restored natural in Mission Hills, preserving the traditional strip-floor appearance while renewing the surface.
caption: Vintage 2¼-inch white oak restored natural in Mission Hills, preserving the traditional strip-floor appearance while renewing the surface.

alt: Restored solid ash flooring installed at 45 degrees in a Del Mar home overlooking Torrey Pines Beach. caption: Another completed view of the Del Mar ash floor, in the main room with sliders opening to the spa deck and the ocean on the horizon. Precision sanding and clear finishing preserve the natural ash grain and diagonal layout.

alt: Vintage Mission Hills white oak flooring after precision sanding and natural clear finishing, seen in the dining area. caption: Another completed view of the Mission Hills vintage white oak floor, in the dining area with its round stone-top pedestal table, looking toward the carved fireplace, after precision sanding and a natural clear finish.

alt: Multiple stain colors sampled on a century-old Mission Hills white oak floor to compare natural and modern restoration options. caption: A broad range of stain colors tested directly on this century-old Mission Hills white oak floor to evaluate how the vintage wood could be renewed while retaining its character.

alt: Engineered oak flooring in the Del Mar–Solana Beach area restored with a custom stain and protective finish. Completed engineered-oak restoration in the Del Mar–Solana Beach area, featuring a custom stain selected for the home.
caption: Completed engineered-oak restoration in the Del Mar–Solana Beach area, featuring a custom stain selected for the home.

alt: Upstairs hardwood flooring in a gated Carmel Valley home restored without sanding through decontamination, water dye, stain and recoating. Cleaning, water dye, stain and a maintenance recoat renewed the floor while preserving the existing wear layer.
caption: Wax-, polish- and grime-contaminated upstairs flooring restored in a gated Carmel Valley home without sanding. Cleaning, water dye, stain and a maintenance recoat renewed the floor while preserving the existing wear layer.

alt: Oak flooring in a La Jolla Shores architectural home with a curved wall of windows overlooking the ocean sunset after deep cleaning, de-waxing and recoating without sanding. caption: Another view of the La Jolla Shores maintenance project at sunset, with the home's curved wall of windows and terrace overlooking the ocean. The oak floor was deep cleaned, de-waxed and recoated without sanding in the expansive multi-level residence.

alt: Engineered walnut flooring being glued to a concrete slab with seams scraped and black French bleed applied before color and finish. alt: Historical Mission Hills white oak sanding project showing older floor-sanding equipment and plastic dust containment used before today's improved systems. San Diego Hardwoods now uses more advanced dust collection and planetary sanding equipment.
caption: Historical restoration view from the Mission Hills white oak project, with an earlier-generation edger and shop vacuum at work between plastic-sheeted cabinets. San Diego Hardwoods now uses more advanced dust collection and planetary sanding equipment.

alt: Historical view from the same Mission Hills white oak restoration, showing the freshly refinished strip flooring in the columned room with French doors. Current projects use improved extraction and more advanced planetary sanding technology.
caption: A second view from the historical Mission Hills restoration, showing the freshly refinished white oak in the columned room opening to the patio. Current projects use improved extraction and more advanced planetary sanding technology.

alt: Traditional red oak flooring in Bird Rock finished with amber oil-based polyurethane in a satin sheen. Traditional Bird Rock red oak finished with oil-based polyurethane in a satin sheen, producing the classic amber tone associated with older hardwood interiors.
